reforms.description: Mexico has launched the first taxonomy in the world that covers both environmental and social objectives, providing transparent definitions of sustainable activities for financial players and companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The taxonomy encourages investment in sustainable activities, potentially leading to increased capital flows towards environmentally and socially responsible projects.
reforms.status_evidence: The taxonomy has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how sustainable finance is defined and regulated, with potential long-term impacts on investment patterns and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico has launched the first world taxonomy that covers both environmental and social activities to provide transparent and consistent definitions to investors.

reforms.description: A new policy package named 'Mano a mano' has been launched to support SMEs' institutional, digital, and financial inclusion, aiming to enhance their participation in international trade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving SMEs' access to resources and markets, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy package has been publicly announced to support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the capabilities of SMEs, which are crucial for the economy, but it is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A new policy package has been launched (Mano a mano, in Spanish) to support SMEs’ institutional, digital and financial inclusion.

reforms.description: Mexico has implemented a four-stage programme to facilitate SMEs becoming local suppliers for foreign direct investment firms, including training and matching processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing SMEs' capabilities to supply larger firms, the programme is expected to increase their productivity and integration into global value chains.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been publicly announced to support SMEs in becoming suppliers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is a significant initiative to enhance SME capabilities but does not represent a fundamental change in policy.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico has put in place a four-stage programme to facilitate SMEs becoming local suppliers of companies growing in Mexico or relocating there.

reforms.description: Mexico's government has reformed its national housing policy to enhance access to adequate housing for all citizens, focusing on low-income households and improving policy coordination.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve living conditions and access to housing, which can enhance overall productivity and social mobility.
reforms.status_evidence: The new housing policy has been put into effect to facilitate access to adequate housing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in housing policy, targeting low-income households and aiming to improve urban living conditions, which is crucial for social equity and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently reformed the national housing policy and its governance to facilitate access to adequate housing to all Mexicans.

reforms.description: The governance and operational rules of the Federal Support Programme for Massive Transport (PROTRAM) are under revision to extend support to smaller municipalities and include transport electrification projects, enhancing the capacity for sustainable urban mobility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving funding mechanisms for sustainable transport, the reform is expected to enhance urban mobility and reduce environmental impacts, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program is currently under revision with expected changes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The revision of PROTRAM represents a significant adjustment to improve urban mobility funding, which is crucial for sustainable development in urban areas.
reforms.source_quote: The governance and operational rules of the urban sustainable mobility program (PROTRAM) are currently under revision.
